b:T477b,<div class="specification">
<p>The diagram shows part of two neurons.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: © International Baccalaureate Organization 2020.]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline how the amphipathic properties of phospholipids play a role in membrane structure.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the role of cholesterol in animal cell membranes.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Describe what happens to the membranes of an animal cell during mitosis.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the name of the structure shown.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>X indicates the movement of a structure in the neuron. Explain what events trigger this movement and what happens next.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.ii.</div>
</div>c:T8b7,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. part hydrophobic/not attracted to water/non-polar <em><strong>AND</strong></em> part hydrophilic/attracted to water/polar;</p>
<p>b. <span style="text-decoration: underline;">bilayer</span> formed (formed naturally by phospholipids in water);</p>
<p>c. <span style="text-decoration: underline;">hydrophilic</span> heads/parts face outwards and <span style="text-decoration: underline;">hydrophobic</span> tails/parts face inwards.</p>
<p><em>Do not allow water loving/hating in mpa or mpc</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. controls/regulates/reduces fluidity of membrane / prevents crystallization of phospholipids;</p>
<p>b. reduces permeability to some substances.</p>
<p><em>Do not accept ‘stabilizes membrane’</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. nuclear membrane breaks down/disappears (in prophase/at start of mitosis);</p>
<p>b. nuclear membrane reforms around two new nuclei (in telophase/at end of mitosis);</p>
<p>c. plasma membrane pulled inwards at equator / cleavage furrow formed;</p>
<p>d. membrane pinches apart to form two cells / cytoplasm divided / cytokinesis.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>synapse/synaptic</p>
<p><em>Allow any answer including either of these terms unless out of context</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. depolarization of pre-synaptic membrane / action potential/nerve impulse arrives;</p>
<p>b. uptake of calcium / calcium ions diffuse in / calcium channels open;</p>
<p>c. structures containing neurotransmitter/vesicles move to/fuse with membrane;</p>
<p>d. <span style="text-decoration: underline;">neurotransmitter</span>/<span style="text-decoration: underline;">acetylcholine</span> released by <span style="text-decoration: underline;">exocytosis</span> into cleft/binds to postsynaptic membrane/receptors;</p>
<p><em>Must see exocytosis</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.ii.</div>
</div>d:T5e0,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Knowledge of the amphipathic properties of phospholipids was generally good.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Half of candidates were able to state the role of cholesterol in animal cell membranes.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>The mark scheme allowed candidates to score marks either for describing changes to the nuclear membrane or the role that the plasma membrane plays in cytokinesis. The best answers referred to both. Many of the candidates who wrote about the nuclear membrane had rather sketchy understanding of how it breaks up into small vesicles at the end of prophase and then reforms around both daughter nuclei in telophase.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>As pointed out on some G2 forms, the question here was rather ambiguous as it wasn’t 100 % clear what structure was referred to, so any answer indicating that the candidate knew the diagram showed a synapse was accepted.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>This was generally well answered. Some suggested that the vesicle is released into the synaptic cleft, instead of neurotransmitter being released by exocytosis.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.ii.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/6/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","6"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"20N.2.HL.TZ0.2"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"20N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","1-3-membrane-structure, 1-6-cell-division, 6-5-neurons-and-synapses"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$d"}}]]}]]}]
